name: David Sanborn
abstract: David William Sanborn (July 30, 1945 – May 12, 2024) was an American alto saxophonist. He worked in many musical genres; his solo recordings typically blended jazz with instrumental pop and R&B.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: Don has played drum set and congas with a lengthy list of jazz and popular artists, including ... David Sanborn
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Sanborn, I played on Dave Sanborn’s first demo and played on his first three albums
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: my old friend Dave Sanborn... So that became an impetus for me to write... eventually we had rehearsals... me and Mike, Sanborn... call the band The Brecker Brothers
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to — and David Sanborn it was like how can David Sanborn and Phil Woods and Cannonball Adderley and Ornette Coleman, how can these all be jazz?
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: we would be on sessions and the saxophones would be Mike Brecker and Ronnie Cuber and Dave Sanborn and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023
